Title: KARRIKIN UP-REGULATED F-BOX 1 (KUF1) imposes negative feedback regulation of karrikin and KAI2 ligand metabolism in Arabidopsis thaliana
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein with N-terminal F-box domain and C-terminal Kelch repeats; predicted beta-propeller
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates seedling photomorphogenesis and root hair development through KAI2 signaling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light seedling growth; KAR1; KAR2; rac-GR24; mock treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by KAR/KL signaling; promoter active in root cap, newly emerged leaves, mature root vasculature, rosette axillary buds, sepals, anthers; low/absent in hypocotyl and root elongation/differentiation zones
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by KAR1 and rac-GR24 in a KAI2- and MAX2-dependent manner; stronger GUS intensity after KAR2 or rac-GR24
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native KUF1 promoter (KUF1p) for rescue/GUS; 35S constitutive promoter for transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray (initial identification among KAR1-upregulated transcripts in dormant seeds)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 loss-of-function mutant; transgenic rescue; qRT-PCR marker analysis; genetic epistasis with kai2/max2/d14; hypocotyl and root hair phenotyping; yeast two-hybrid with ASK1; SMAX1 reporter assays; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of KUF1 causes constitutive KAI2/KL-like responses, including shorter hypocotyls, larger cotyledons, altered marker gene expression, and increased root hair density/elongation. 2. kuf1 phenotypes require KAI2 and MAX2, placing KUF1 upstream of the KAI2-SCFMAX2 pathway. 3. kuf1 specifically enhances sensitivity to KAR1 but not rac-GR24, supporting a role in regulating KAR1 metabolism rather than general pathway sensitivity. 4. Indirect assays support that KUF1 negatively regulates endogenous KL abundance and KAR1 conversion to a bioactive signal. 5. KUF1 likely functions in an SCF-type E3 ligase feedback loop induced by KAI2 signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of KL biosynthesis/availability and KAR1 metabolism; loss of function enhances KAI2-dependent developmental responses and KAR1 sensitivity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate KAI2-mediated seedling vigor, root hair traits, and aid KL discovery; caution because effects are pathway- and ligand-specific
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The direct biochemical substrate/target of SCF^KUF1 is unknown; metabolite identities of KL and KAR1-derived active product are unknown; overexpression effects in Nicotiana differ from Arabidopsis, suggesting species/context dependence

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: alpha/beta-hydrolase with conserved Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: KAI2 ligand (KL); KAR1-derived bioactive signal; rac-GR24/GR24ent-5DS-responsive signal
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls seedling photomorphogenesis and root hair development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: KAR1; KAR2; rac-GR24; red light; mock treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in Arabidopsis; AtKAI2 reporter constructs and KAI2 promoter in transgenic contexts mentioned
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: KL; KAR1-derived active metabolite; KAR2-derived signal; GR24ent-5DS/rac-GR24
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal perception controlling KL/KAR response output
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-dependent activation leading to interaction with SCFMAX2 and degradation of SMAX1/SMXL2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in feedback loop through induction of KUF1 expression after signaling activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of SMAX1 and SMXL2, thereby activating downstream KAR/KL-responsive gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic epistasis (kai2 suppresses kuf1); protein abundance assays; reporter assays for SMAX1 degradation; pharmacological response assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAI2 is required for kuf1 phenotypes, showing KUF1 acts upstream of KAI2 signaling. 2. KAI2 protein abundance is not increased in kuf1, excluding KUF1 action through KAI2 stabilization. 3. kuf1 specifically alters perception of KAR1 but not rac-GR24, implying KAI2 receives distinct ligand inputs depending on metabolite processing. 4. KAI2-dependent root hair and hypocotyl responses are enhanced in kuf1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for enhanced constitutive and KAR1-hypersensitive responses in kuf1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant target for engineering KAI2-dependent developmental responses, but ligand specificity and pathway context are critical
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the pathway, KAI2 is not the primary novel gene under study; included because results directly test its necessity for KUF1-linked metabolite effects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential receptor-like signaling component required for perception of KL and KAR-derived signals driving the phenotype

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: F-box protein of SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls seedling growth and root hair development via KAI2 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction and proteolysis downstream of KAI2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: SCF-mediated polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of repressors
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Supports feedback by enabling KUF1 induction after signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of SMAX1/SMXL2, permitting KAR/KL-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic epistasis (max2 suppresses kuf1); pathway phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAX2 is necessary for kuf1 constitutive phenotypes, placing KUF1 upstream of MAX2-dependent signaling. 2. The study supports a negative feedback circuit in which KUF1 induction depends on KAI2-SCFMAX2 pathway activity. 3. MAX2-dependent processes such as hypocotyl inhibition and root hair promotion are enhanced in kuf1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for kuf1-driven enhancement of KAI2-mediated responses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central leverage point for KAI2/KAR signaling manipulation, but pleiotropic signaling roles may complicate deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also functions in strigolactone signaling, but d14 epistasis indicates kuf1 effects in this paper are specific to the KAI2 branch
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential SCF signaling component required for KUF1-associated regulation of KL/KAR1 response output
